features.claim: Antigravity lets users run custom scripts or shell commands at specific points during its execution loop to enforce rules, run linters, or capture diagnostics.
features.name: Hooks
features.quote: Hooks allow you to run custom scripts or shell commands at specific points during Antigravity's execution loop. This is powerful for enforcing custom rules, running linters, or capturing diagnostics automatically.

features.claim: A hook event that fires before a tool is executed, allowing scripts to gate, block, or prompt the user before any tool call proceeds.
features.name: PreToolUse
features.quote: PreToolUse | Fires before a tool is executed. | Tool name (e.g., run_command)

features.claim: A hook event that fires after a tool completes, enabling post-execution scripts such as linters or diagnostic capture.
features.name: PostToolUse
features.quote: PostToolUse | Fires after a tool completes. | Tool name

features.claim: A hook event that fires before the model is called, with the ability to inject steps (tool calls, user messages, or ephemeral messages) into the conversation trajectory.
features.name: PreInvocation
features.quote: PreInvocation | Fires before the model is called. | N/A (matcher ignored)

features.claim: A hook event that fires after tool calls finish and can inject steps or control execution flow by forcing continuation or termination of the loop.
features.name: PostInvocation
features.quote: PostInvocation | Fires after tool calls finish. | N/A (matcher ignored)

features.claim: A hook event that fires when the execution loop terminates, allowing a script to inspect the termination reason and optionally force the agent to re-enter the execution loop.
features.name: Stop
features.quote: Stop | Fires when execution terminates. | N/A (matcher ignored)
